1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"

8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45	
46		// Claim
47		Claim(id, assignee string) error
48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
55	
56		// Dep ops
57		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
60	
61		// Events
62		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
63		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
64	
65		// Archive split
66		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
67		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
68	
69		// JSONL interchange
70		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
71		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
72	}
73

75	// change the legacy Store-backed interface above in this bead slice.

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19	
20	import (
21		"bufio"
22		"bytes"
23		"context"
24		"crypto/rand"
25		"encoding/hex"
26		"encoding/json"
27		"fmt"
28		"os"
29		"path/filepath"
30		"sort"
31		"strconv"
32		"strings"
33		"time"
34	
35		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
36		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
37	)
38	
39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,
101			LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
102		}
103		for _, opt := range opts {
104			if opt != nil {
105				opt(s)
106			}
107		}
108		spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
109		s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)
110	
111		// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
112		// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
113		// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
114		// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
115		// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
116		// import/export round-trip).
117		switch backendType {
118		case BackendBD, BackendBR:
119			var fallback RawBackend
120			if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
121				fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
122			}
123			if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
124				s.backend = ext
125			}
126			// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
127		case BackendAxon:
128			s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
129		}
130	
131		return s
132	}
133	
134	func storeCallContext(args []any) context.Context {
135		for _, arg := range args {
136			if ctx, ok := arg.(context.Context); ok && ctx != nil {
137				return ctx
138			}
139		}
140		return context.Background()
141	}
142	
143	// NewStoreWithCollection creates a store for a named logical collection.
144	func NewStoreWithCollection(dir, collection string) *Store {
145		return NewStore(dir, WithCollection(collection))
146	}
147	
148	// Init creates the storage directory and file.
149	func (s *Store) Init(args ...any) error {
150		if err := storeCallContext(args).Err(); err != nil {
151			return err
152		}
153		if s.backend != nil {
154			return s.backend.Init()
155		}
156		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
157			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
158		}
159		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
160		if err != nil {
161			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
162		}
163		return f.Close()
164	}
165	
166	// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
167	func (s *Store) GenID(args ...any) (string, error) {
168		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
169		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
170			return "", err
171		}
172		prefix := s.Prefix
173		if prefix == "" {
174			prefix = DefaultIDPrefix
175		} else if !strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "-") {
176			prefix += "-"
177		}
178		return RandomHexIDGenerator{Prefix: prefix, Bytes: 4}.GenID(ctx)
179	}
180	
181	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
182	func (s *Store) ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
183		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
184		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
185			return nil, err
186		}
187		if s.backend != nil {
188			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
189			if err != nil {
190				return nil, err
191			}
192			return all, nil
193		}
194		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
195		if err != nil {
196			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
197		}
198		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
199		for _, warning := range warnings {
200			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
201		}
202		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
203			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
204			if repairErr != nil {
205				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
206			}
207			if repaired {
208				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
209			}
210		}
211		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
212			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
213		}
214		return beads, nil
215	}
216	
217	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
218	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
219	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
220	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
221	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
222	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
223	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
224	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error) {
225		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
226		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
227			return nil, err
228		}
229		var pred func(Bead) bool
230		for _, arg := range args {
231			if fn, ok := arg.(func(Bead) bool); ok {
232				pred = fn
233				break
234			}
235		}
236		if s.backend != nil {
237			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
238			if err != nil {
239				return nil, err
240			}
241			if pred == nil {
242				return all, nil
243			}
244			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
245			for _, b := range all {
246				if pred(b) {
247					out = append(out, b)
248				}
249			}
250			return out, nil
251		}
252		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
253		if err != nil {
254			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
255		}
256		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
257		for _, warning := range warnings {
258			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
259		}
260		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
261			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
262			if repairErr != nil {
263				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
264			}
265			if repaired {
266				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
267			}
268		}
269		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
270			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
271		}
272		if pred == nil {
273			return beads, nil
274		}
275		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
276		for _, b := range beads {
277			if pred(b) {
278				out = append(out, b)
279			}
280		}
281		return out, nil
282	}
283	
284	func (s *Store) readAllRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
285		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
286		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
287			return nil, nil, nil
288		}
289		if err != nil {
290			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("read: %w", err)
291		}
292		beads, warnings, err := parseBeadJSONL(data)
293		if err != nil {
294			return nil, nil, err
295		}
296		return beads, warnings, nil
297	}
298	
299	func parseBeadJSONL(data []byte) ([]Bead, []string, error) {
300		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(data))
301		// 16MB max line. Real-world extremes observed: 1.46MB bead rows when a
302		// reviewer stream dumped into events[].body, and bd-exported lines stacking
303		// many 65KB fields. bd's upstream per-field cap is 65,535 bytes; an
304		// individual bead with dozens of maxed-out fields fits comfortably in 16MB.
305		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
306	
307		var beads []Bead
308		var warnings []string
309		lineNo := 0
310		for scanner.Scan() {
311			lineNo++
312			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
313			if line == "" {
314				continue
315			}
316			b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
317			if err != nil {
318				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("bead: read line %d: %v", lineNo, err))
319				continue
320			}
321			beads = append(beads, b)
322		}
323		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
324			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("scan jsonl: %w", err)
325		}
326		return beads, warnings, nil
327	}
328	
329	func (s *Store) repairJSONL() (bool, error) {
330		var repaired bool
331		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
332			beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
333			if err != nil {
334				return err
335			}
336			beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
337			if len(warnings) == 0 || len(beads) == 0 {
338				return nil
339			}
340			backupPath := s.File + ".bak"
341			backupData, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
342			if err != nil {
343				return fmt.Errorf("read current file: %w", err)
344			}
345			if err := writeAtomicFile(backupPath, backupData); err != nil {
346				return fmt.Errorf("write backup: %w", err)
347			}
348			if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
349				return fmt.Errorf("write repaired file: %w", err)
350			}
351			repaired = true
352			return nil
353		})
354		return repaired, err
355	}
356	
357	func foldLatestBeads(beads []Bead) []Bead {
358		if len(beads) == 0 {
359			return nil
360		}
361	
362		latest := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
363		lastSeen := make(map[string]int, len(beads))
364		for i, b := range beads {
365			latest[b.ID] = b
366			lastSeen[b.ID] = i
367		}
368	
369		ids := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
370		for id := range latest {
371			ids = append(ids, id)
372		}
373		sort.Slice(ids, func(i, j int) bool {
374			if lastSeen[ids[i]] != lastSeen[ids[j]] {
375				return lastSeen[ids[i]] < lastSeen[ids[j]]
376			}
377			return ids[i] < ids[j]
378		})
379	
380		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(ids))
381		for _, id := range ids {
382			out = append(out, latest[id])
383		}
384		return out
385	}
386	
387	func (s *Store) readAllLatestRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
388		// When a non-JSONL backend is configured, the raw bead corpus lives
389		// outside .ddx/<collection>.jsonl entirely (axon: two collections under
390		// .ddx/axon/; bd/br: an external tool's store). Delegate the read so
391		// every read-modify-write path inside Store stays consistent with the
392		// backend's storage layout. Backends are responsible for returning
393		// already-folded beads.
394		if s.backend != nil {
395			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
396			if err != nil {
397				return nil, nil, err
398			}
399			return all, nil, nil
400		}
401		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
402		if err != nil {
403			return nil, nil, err
404		}
405		return foldLatestBeads(beads), warnings, nil
406	}
407	
408	// tmpPath returns a unique temp file path in the same directory as path.
409	// Uses pid + 4 random bytes so concurrent processes don't collide.
410	func tmpPath(path string) (string, error) {
411		b := make([]byte, 4)
412		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
413			return "", err
414		}
415		return fmt.Sprintf("%s.tmp-%d-%s", path, os.Getpid(), hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil
416	}
417	
418	func writeAtomicFile(path string, data []byte) error {
419		tmp, err := tmpPath(path)
420		if err != nil {
421			return err
422		}
423		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
424		if err != nil {
425			return err
426		}
427		if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
428			f.Close()
429			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
430			return err
431		}
432		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
433			f.Close()
434			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
435			return err
436		}
437		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
438			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
439			return err
440		}
441		if err := os.Rename(tmp, path); err != nil {
442			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
443			return err
444		}
445		return nil
446	}
447	
448	func normalizeCollection(name string) string {
449		name = strings.TrimSpace(name)
450		if name == "" {
451			return DefaultCollection
452		}
453		name = strings.TrimSuffix(name, ".jsonl")
454		return name
455	}
456	
457	// WriteAll writes all beads to the configured backend, sorted by ID.
458	func (s *Store) WriteAll(beads []Bead) error {
459		sort.Slice(beads, func(i, j int) bool {
460			return beads[i].ID < beads[j].ID
461		})
462	
463		if s.backend != nil {
464			return s.backend.WriteAll(beads)
465		}
466	
467		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
468			return fmt.Errorf("bead: mkdir: %w", err)
469		}
470	
471		tmp, err := tmpPath(s.File)
472		if err != nil {
473			return fmt.Errorf("bead: tmp name: %w", err)
474		}
475		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
476		if err != nil {
477			return fmt.Errorf("bead: create tmp: %w", err)
478		}
479	
480		enc := json.NewEncoder(f)
481		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
482		for _, b := range beads {
483			data, err := marshalBead(b)
484			if err != nil {
485				f.Close()
486				os.Remove(tmp)
487				return fmt.Errorf("bead: marshal: %w", err)
488			}
489			if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
490				f.Close()
491				os.Remove(tmp)
492				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write: %w", err)
493			}
494			if _, err := f.WriteString("\n"); err != nil {
495				f.Close()
496				os.Remove(tmp)
497				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write newline: %w", err)
498			}
499		}
500	
501		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
502			f.Close()
503			os.Remove(tmp)
504			return fmt.Errorf("bead: sync tmp: %w", err)
505		}
506		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
507			os.Remove(tmp)
508			return fmt.Errorf("bead: close tmp: %w", err)
509		}
510		if err := os.Rename(tmp, s.File); err != nil {
511			os.Remove(tmp)
512			return fmt.Errorf("bead: rename tmp: %w", err)
513		}
514		return nil
515	}
516	
517	// Create adds a new bead. Assigns defaults, validates, then persists.
518	func (s *Store) Create(args ...any) error {
519		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
520		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
521			return err
522		}
523		var b *Bead
524		for _, arg := range args {
525			if bead, ok := arg.(*Bead); ok {
526				b = bead
527				break
528			}
529		}
530		if b == nil {
531			return fmt.Errorf("bead: create requires bead")
532		}
533		now := time.Now().UTC()
534		if b.ID == "" {
535			id, err := s.GenID(ctx)
536			if err != nil {
537				return err
538			}
539			b.ID = id
540		}
541		if b.IssueType == "" {
542			b.IssueType = DefaultType
543		}
544		if b.Status == "" {
545			// Create-time StatusOpen assignment is initial state, not a lifecycle transition.
546			// This is the sole exception to ValidateLifecycleTransition: all other status
547			// writes go through transitionLifecycleInPlace, which validates transitions via
548			// ValidateLifecycleTransition (TD-031 §3 transition matrix pattern).
549			b.Status = DefaultStatus
550		}
551		b.CreatedAt = now
552		b.UpdatedAt = now
553	
554		// Validate after defaults are applied so hooks see final state
555		if err := s.validateBead(b); err != nil {
556			return err
557		}
558		// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create another
559		// operator-prompt bead. The execute-bead harness exports the actor's
560		// issue_type via DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE; absent the env, the guard is a
561		// no-op (e.g. direct CLI use by a human operator).
562		if err := OperatorPromptMutationGuard(os.Getenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE"), b.IssueType); err != nil {
563			return err
564		}
565		// Run create hook
566		if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-create", b); err != nil {
567			return err
568		}
569	
570		return s.WithLock(func() error {
571			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
572			if err != nil {
573				return err
574			}
575			// Reject duplicate IDs
576			for _, e := range beads {
577				if e.ID == b.ID {
578					return fmt.Errorf("bead: duplicate id: %s", b.ID)
579				}
580			}
581			// Validate deps reference existing beads
582			depIDs := b.DepIDs()
583			if len(depIDs) > 0 {
584				existing := make(map[string]bool)
585				for _, e := range beads {
586					existing[e.ID] = true
587				}
588				for _, dep := range depIDs {
589					if !existing[dep] {
590						return fmt.Errorf("bead: dependency not found: %s", dep)
591					}
592				}
593			}
594			beads = append(beads, *b)
595			return s.WriteAll(beads)
596		})
597	}
598	
599	// Get retrieves a bead by ID.
600	func (s *Store) Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error) {
601		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
602		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
603			return nil, err
604		}
605		var id string
606		for _, arg := range args {
607			if s, ok := arg.(string); ok {
608				id = s
609				break
610			}
611		}
612		if id == "" {
613			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: get requires bead id")
614		}
615		beads, err := s.ReadAll(args...)
616		if err != nil {
617			return nil, err
618		}
619		for _, b := range beads {
620			if b.ID == id {
621				return &b, nil
622			}
623		}
624		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
625	}
626	
627	// Update modifies a bead by ID. The mutate function receives a pointer to
628	// modify, but persisted lifecycle status changes must use TransitionLifecycle.
629	func (s *Store) Update(args ...any) error {
630		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
631		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
632			return err
633		}
634		var id string
635		var mutate func(*Bead)
636		for _, arg := range args {
637			switch v := arg.(type) {
638			case string:
639				if id == "" {
640					id = v
641				}
642			case func(*Bead):
643				mutate = v
644			}
645		}
646		if id == "" {
647			return fmt.Errorf("bead: update requires bead id")
648		}
649		if mutate == nil {
650			return fmt.Errorf("bead: update requires mutate func")
651		}
652		return s.updateBead(id, false, func(b *Bead) error {
653			mutate(b)
654			return nil
655		})
656	}
657	
658	// Apply mutates a bead through a typed Operation. When the configured raw
659	// backend exposes the optional OperationApplier fast path, Apply delegates to
660	// it; otherwise it falls back to the generic load-mutate-save path.
661	func (s *Store) Apply(id string, op Operation) error {
662		if s == nil {
663			return fmt.Errorf("bead: nil store")
664		}
665		if op == nil {
666			return fmt.Errorf("bead: nil operation")
667		}
668		if applier, ok := s.backend.(OperationApplier); ok {
669			return applier.Apply(context.Background(), id, op)
670		}
671		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
672			return op.Apply(b)
673		})
674	}
675	
676	func (s *Store) updateBead(id string, allowStatusChange bool, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
677		return s.WithLock(func() error {
678			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
679			if err != nil {
680				return err
681			}
682			found := false
683			for i := range beads {
684				if beads[i].ID == id {
685					beforeStatus := beads[i].Status
686					if mutate != nil {
687						if err := mutate(&beads[i]); err != nil {
688							return err
689						}
690					}
691					if !allowStatusChange && beads[i].Status != beforeStatus {
692						return fmt.Errorf("bead: lifecycle status change %s -> %s requires Store.TransitionLifecycle", beforeStatus, beads[i].Status)
693					}
694					beads[i].UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
695					// Core validation after mutation
696					if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
697						return err
698					}
699					// Run update hook
700					if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
701						return err
702					}
703					found = true
704					break
705				}
706			}
707			if !found {
708				return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
709			}
710			return s.WriteAll(beads)
711		})
712	}
713	
714	// TransitionLifecycle validates and persists one bead lifecycle status
715	// transition. Callers may provide a mutator for metadata that must land
716	// atomically with the status write.
717	func (s *Store) TransitionLifecycle(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
718		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
719			beforeStatus := b.Status
720			if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, status, opts); err != nil {
721				return err
722			}
723			if mutate != nil {
724				return mutate(b)
725			}
726			appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b, beforeStatus, status, opts)
727			return nil
728		})
729	}
730	
731	// SetLifecycleStatus validates and persists a lifecycle status transition.
732	func (s *Store) SetLifecycleStatus(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) error {
733		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, status, opts, nil)
734	}
735	
736	// UpdateWithLifecycleStatus atomically applies non-status field mutations and
737	// a validated lifecycle status transition.
738	func (s *Store) UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
739		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
740			beforeStatus := b.Status
741			if mutate != nil {
742				if err := mutate(b); err != nil {
743					return err
744				}
745			}
746			if b.Status != beforeStatus {
747				return fmt.Errorf("bead: UpdateWithLifecycleStatus mutator changed lifecycle status directly")
748			}
749			if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, status, opts); err != nil {
750				return err
751			}
752			appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b, beforeStatus, status, opts)
753			return nil
754		})
755	}
756	
757	func transitionLifecycleInPlace(b *Bead, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) error {
758		from := b.Status
759		if err := ValidateLifecycleTransition(from, status, opts); err != nil {
760			return fmt.Errorf("bead: lifecycle transition %s -> %s rejected: %w", from, status, err)
761		}
762		b.Status = status
763		applyLifecycleTransitionMetadata(b, from, status, opts)
764		return nil
765	}
766	
767	const ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason = "lifecycle-external-blocker-reason"
768	
769	// ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker is the bead Extra key holding the consecutive-wedge
770	// count recorded by the execute-bead loop. It is cleared at the store layer on
771	// any transition to StatusOpen so that operator-reopened beads get a fresh
772	// attempt rather than being instantly re-parked by the guard (ddx-5c549120).
773	const ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker = "work-consecutive-wedges"
774	
775	func applyLifecycleTransitionMetadata(b *Bead, from, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) {
776		if b.Extra == nil {
777			b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
778		}
779		if status == StatusBlocked {
780			if reason := strings.TrimSpace(opts.ExternalBlockerReason); reason != "" {
781				b.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason] = reason
782			}
783		} else {
784			delete(b.Extra, ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason)
785		}
786		// Clear stale consecutive-wedge marker on operator reopen (proposed/blocked →
787		// open) so the execute-bead loop gets a fresh attempt rather than re-parking
788		// without trying (ddx-5c549120). Only clears on operator-facing transitions;
789		// internal release (in_progress → open) preserves the count.
790		if status == StatusOpen && (from == StatusProposed || from == StatusBlocked) {
791			delete(b.Extra, ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker)
792		}
793	}
794	
795	func appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b *Bead, from, to string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) {
796		if b == nil || from != StatusProposed || to != StatusOpen {
797			return
798		}
799		acceptedFingerprint, acceptedPromptFingerprint := latestParkedIntakeFingerprint(b)
800		body := map[string]any{
801			"from_status":                 from,
802			"to_status":                   to,
803			"reason":                      strings.TrimSpace(opts.Reason),
804			"source":                      strings.TrimSpace(opts.Source),
805			"operator_required":           true,
806			"accepted_fingerprint":        acceptedFingerprint,
807			"accepted_prompt_fingerprint": acceptedPromptFingerprint,
808		}
809		if actor := strings.TrimSpace(opts.Actor); actor != "" {
810			body["actor"] = actor
811		}
812		if promptFingerprint := PromptFingerprint(*b); promptFingerprint != "" {
813			body["prompt_fingerprint"] = promptFingerprint
814		}
815		payload, _ := json.Marshal(body)
816		appendInlineEvent(b, BeadEvent{
817			Kind:      "triaged",
818			Summary:   "operator accepted proposed bead",
819			Body:      string(payload),
820			Actor:     strings.TrimSpace(opts.Actor),
821			Source:    strings.TrimSpace(opts.Source),
822			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
823		})
824	}
825	
826	func latestParkedIntakeFingerprint(b *Bead) (string, string) {
827		if b == nil || b.Extra == nil {
828			return "", ""
829		}
830		events := decodeBeadEvents(b.Extra["events"])
831		for i := len(events) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
832			switch events[i].Kind {
833			case "intake.blocked", "pre_claim_intake.blocked":
834				fields := beadEventBodyFields(events[i].Body)
835				fingerprint := strings.TrimSpace(fields["fingerprint"])
836				if fingerprint == "" {
837					continue
838				}
839				return fingerprint, strings.TrimSpace(fields["prompt_fingerprint"])
840			}
841		}
842		return "", ""
843	}
844	
845	func beadEventBodyFields(body string) map[string]string {
846		fields := map[string]string{}
847		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
848			return fields
849		}
850		var raw map[string]any
851		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(body), &raw); err != nil {
852			return fields
853		}
854		for k, v := range raw {
855			if s, ok := v.(string); ok {
856				fields[k] = s
857			}
858		}
859		return fields
860	}
861	
862	// HeartbeatInterval is how often a claim owner should refresh the external
863	// claim liveness lease while running. Exposed as a variable so tests can
864	// override it.
865	var HeartbeatInterval = 30 * time.Second
866	
867	// HeartbeatTTL is the maximum allowed age of the external claim liveness
868	// lease before another worker may reclaim the bead. Defaults to 3×
869	// HeartbeatInterval. Exposed as a variable so tests can override it.
870	var HeartbeatTTL = 90 * time.Second
871	
872	// Claim sets a bead to in_progress with claim metadata.
873	// Fails if the bead is already claimed (status == in_progress) with a
874	// fresh claim lease. A bead whose external lease is older than HeartbeatTTL
875	// is considered stalled and will be reclaimed atomically.
876	func (s *Store) Claim(id, assignee string) error {
877		machine, _ := os.Hostname()
878		if envID := os.Getenv("DDX_MACHINE_ID"); envID != "" {
879			machine = envID
880		}
881		return s.WithLock(func() error {
882			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
883			if err != nil {
884				return err
885			}
886			for i := range beads {
887				if beads[i].ID != id {
888					continue
889				}
890				switch beads[i].Status {
891				case StatusOpen:
892					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
893						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
894					}
895					// normal claim path
896				case StatusInProgress:
897					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
898						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
899					}
900					// stalled claim — reclaim atomically below
901				default:
902					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
903				}
904				if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(&beads[i], StatusInProgress, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
905					Reason: "claim",
906					Actor:  assignee,
907					Source: "Store.Claim",
908				}); err != nil {
909					return err
910				}
911				now := time.Now().UTC()
912				beads[i].Owner = assignee
913				beads[i].UpdatedAt = now
914				if beads[i].Extra == nil {
915					beads[i].Extra = make(map[string]any)
916				}
917				// Compatibility cleanup only: worker/live claim metadata now lives in
918				// the external lease sidecar rather than the tracked row.
919				for _, key := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
920					delete(beads[i].Extra, key)
921				}
922				delete(beads[i].Extra, ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey)
923				if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
924					return err
925				}
926				if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
927					return err
928				}
929				if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
930					return err
931				}
932				return s.writeClaimHeartbeat(ClaimLeaseRecord{
933					BeadID:    id,
934					Owner:     assignee,
935					Machine:   machine,
936					StartedAt: now,
937					UpdatedAt: now,
938					PID:       os.Getpid(),
939				})
940			}
941			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
942		})
943	}
944	
945	// ClaimWithOptions acquires or refreshes the external worker-claim lease
946	// without mutating the tracked bead row. session and worktree are optional;
947	// machine is derived from os.Hostname(). A stalled open/in_progress bead
948	// (external lease older than HeartbeatTTL) is reclaimed atomically under the
949	// store's lock.
950	func (s *Store) ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error {
951		machine, _ := os.Hostname()
952		if envID := os.Getenv("DDX_MACHINE_ID"); envID != "" {
953			machine = envID
954		}
955		return s.WithLock(func() error {
956			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
957			if err != nil {
958				return err
959			}
960			for i := range beads {
961				if beads[i].ID != id {
962					continue
963				}
964				switch beads[i].Status {
965				case StatusOpen:
966					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
967						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
968					}
969					// worker lease path
970				case StatusInProgress:
971					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
972						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
973					}
974					// stalled claim — reclaim atomically below
975				default:
976					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
977				}
978				now := time.Now().UTC()
979				return s.writeClaimHeartbeat(ClaimLeaseRecord{
980					BeadID:    id,
981					Owner:     assignee,
982					Session:   session,
983					Worktree:  worktree,
984					Machine:   machine,
985					StartedAt: now,
986					UpdatedAt: now,
987					PID:       os.Getpid(),
988				})
989			}
990			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
991		})
992	}
993	
994	// Heartbeat refreshes the external claim liveness lease on a claimed bead.
995	// Returns an error if the bead is no longer in_progress (e.g., reclaimed by
996	// another worker), allowing the caller to stop its heartbeat loop.
997	//
998	// Deprecated: use TouchClaimHeartbeat.
999	func (s *Store) Heartbeat(id string) error {
1000		if err := s.WithLock(func() error {
1001			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1002			if err != nil {
1003				return err
1004			}
1005			for _, b := range beads {
1006				if b.ID != id {
1007					continue
1008				}
1009				if b.Status != StatusInProgress {
1010					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot heartbeat %s from status %s", id, b.Status)
1011				}
1012				return s.TouchClaimHeartbeat(id)
1013			}
1014			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
1015		}); err != nil {
1016			return err
1017		}
1018		return nil
1019	}
1020	
1021	// claimLeaseIsStale returns true if the external claim lease is absent or
1022	// older than HeartbeatTTL. When no lease file exists, it falls back to the
1023	// legacy tracker heartbeat field for compatibility with imported/stale rows.
1024	func claimLeaseIsStale(s *Store, extra map[string]any, id string) bool {
1025		if s != nil {
1026			if fresh, found, err := s.ClaimHeartbeatFresh(id); err == nil {
1027				if found {
1028					return !fresh
1029				}
1030			} else {
1031				return heartbeatIsStale(extra)
1032			}
1033		}
1034		return heartbeatIsStale(extra)
1035	}
1036	
1037	// heartbeatIsStale returns true if the given bead's legacy Extra map has no
1038	// heartbeat field or one older than HeartbeatTTL.
1039	func heartbeatIsStale(extra map[string]any) bool {
1040		if extra == nil {
1041			return true
1042		}
1043		raw, ok := extra["work-heartbeat-at"]
1044		if !ok {
1045			return true
1046		}
1047		s, ok := raw.(string)
1048		if !ok || s == "" {
1049			return true
1050		}
1051		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)
1052		if err != nil {
1053			// Fall back to RFC3339 for compatibility with legacy entries written
1054			// before sub-second resolution. RFC3339Nano is the current canonical
1055			// format used by both ClaimWithOptions and Heartbeat write sites.
1056			t, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s)
1057			if err != nil {
1058				return true
1059			}
1060		}
1061		return time.Since(t) > HeartbeatTTL
1062	}
1063	
1064	// clearClaimExtraKeys resets claim state by deleting all claim-related metadata
1065	// from the Extra map. It clears both legacy claim metadata (claimed-at, claimed-pid,
1066	// claimed-machine, claimed-session, claimed-worktree) and the claim heartbeat lease
1067	// timestamp. These keys are cleared when a bead is unclaimed or reopened to fully
1068	// reset its claim state so it can be claimed again by another worker.
1069	func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) {
1070		if extra == nil {
1071			return
1072		}
1073		for _, k := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
1074			delete(extra, k)
1075		}
1076		delete(extra, ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey)
1077	}
1078	
1079	// Unclaim clears claim metadata. Only reverts status to open if the bead
1080	// is currently in_progress — a closed bead stays closed.
1081	func (s *Store) Unclaim(id string) error {
1082		if err := s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
1083			if b.Status == StatusInProgress {
1084				if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, StatusOpen, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1085					Reason: "unclaim",
1086					Source: "Store.Unclaim",
1087				}); err != nil {
1088					return err
1089				}
1090			}
1091			b.Owner = ""
1092			clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra)
1093			return nil
1094		}); err != nil {
1095			return err
1096		}
1097		return s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1098	}
1099	
1100	// Release is the atomic, symmetric counterpart to Claim: it transitions a
1101	// claimed bead back to targetStatus (StatusOpen when empty), clears Owner,
1102	// persists the row, AND removes the external claim-heartbeat lease sidecar —
1103	// all inside a single WithLock critical section. Unlike Unclaim (which removes
1104	// the lease only after the lock is released), Release couples claim clearing
1105	// and lifecycle status in one locked write, so a failure-release can never
1106	// leave a stale lease sidecar while `ddx bead status` reports the bead as not
1107	// in_progress (the phantom-lease inconsistency observed in ddx-8f2e0ebf).
1108	//
1109	// Only an in_progress bead has its status transitioned; a bead in any other
1110	// status (e.g. closed) keeps its status and only has Owner and the lease
1111	// cleared, mirroring Unclaim's guard so a released bead is never reopened.
1112	func (s *Store) Release(id, assignee, targetStatus string) error {
1113		if strings.TrimSpace(targetStatus) == "" {
1114			targetStatus = StatusOpen
1115		}
1116		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1117			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1118			if err != nil {
1119				return err
1120			}
1121			for i := range beads {
1122				if beads[i].ID != id {
1123					continue
1124				}
1125				if beads[i].Status == StatusInProgress {
1126					if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(&beads[i], targetStatus, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1127						Reason: "release",
1128						Actor:  assignee,
1129						Source: "Store.Release",
1130					}); err != nil {
1131						return err
1132					}
1133				}
1134				beads[i].Owner = ""
1135				beads[i].UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
1136				clearClaimExtraKeys(beads[i].Extra)
1137				if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
1138					return err
1139				}
1140				if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
1141					return err
1142				}
1143				if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
1144					return err
1145				}
1146				return s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1147			}
1148			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
1149		})
1150	}
1151	
1152	// SetExecutionCooldown parks bead id until the given wall-clock time.
1153	// baseRev is the origin/main HEAD at cooldown time; when non-empty it enables
1154	// rev-bound auto-invalidation: if origin/main advances past baseRev the
1155	// cooldown clears automatically on the next ready-queue pass.
1156	func (s *Store) SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail, baseRev string) error {
1157		return s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1158			if b.Extra == nil {
1159				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1160			}
1161			b.Extra["work-retry-after"] = until.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
1162			if status != "" {
1163				b.Extra["work-last-status"] = status
1164			}
1165			if detail != "" {
1166				b.Extra["work-last-detail"] = detail
1167			}
1168			if baseRev != "" {
1169				b.Extra[ExtraCooldownBaseRev] = baseRev
1170			} else {
1171				delete(b.Extra, ExtraCooldownBaseRev)
1172			}
1173		})
1174	}
1175	
1176	// ClearCooldowns clears work cooldown fields for all beads matched by
1177	// filter. If filter is nil, every bead with work-retry-after set is
1178	// cleared. Returns the count of beads whose cooldowns were cleared. A
1179	// "cooldown-cleared" event with reason="operator-bulk-clear" is appended for
1180	// each cleared bead in the same pass.
1181	func (s *Store) ClearCooldowns(filter func(Bead) bool) (int, error) {
1182		var count int
1183		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
1184			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1185			if err != nil {
1186				return err
1187			}
1188			now := time.Now().UTC()
1189			for i := range beads {
1190				b := &beads[i]
1191				if extraStringVal(b.Extra, ExtraRetryAfter) == "" {
1192					continue
1193				}
1194				if filter != nil && !filter(*b) {
1195					continue
1196				}
1197				if b.Extra == nil {
1198					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1199				}
1200				delete(b.Extra, ExtraRetryAfter)
1201				delete(b.Extra, ExtraCooldownBaseRev)
1202				delete(b.Extra, ExtraLastStatus)
1203				delete(b.Extra, ExtraLastDetail)
1204				b.UpdatedAt = now
1205				var events []BeadEvent
1206				if raw, ok := b.Extra["events"]; ok {
1207					events = decodeBeadEvents(raw)
1208				}
1209				events = append(events, BeadEvent{
1210					Kind:      "cooldown-cleared",
1211					Summary:   "operator-bulk-clear",
1212					Source:    "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
1213					CreatedAt: now,
1214				})
1215				b.Extra["events"] = encodeEventsForExtra(events)
1216				if err := s.validateBead(b); err != nil {
1217					return err
1218				}
1219				count++
1220			}
1221			if count == 0 {
1222				return nil
1223			}
1224			return s.WriteAll(beads)
1225		})
1226		return count, err
1227	}
1228	
1229	// IncrNoChangesCount increments the work no-changes counter for a bead
1230	// and returns the new count. Used by the execute-bead worker to detect when a
1231	// bead should be auto-closed after repeated no-change attempts.
1232	func (s *Store) IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error) {
1233		var newCount int
1234		err := s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1235			if b.Extra == nil {
1236				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1237			}
1238			var count int
1239			if v, ok := b.Extra["work-no-changes-count"]; ok {
1240				switch n := v.(type) {
1241				case int:
1242					count = n
1243				case float64:
1244					count = int(n)
1245				case int64:
1246					count = int(n)
1247				}
1248			}
1249			count++
1250			b.Extra["work-no-changes-count"] = count
1251			newCount = count
1252		})
1253		return newCount, err
1254	}
1255	
1256	// MaxFieldBytes is the per-field hard cap on bead event bodies and adjacent
1257	// writer paths. 65,535 bytes matches upstream bd's Dolt TEXT column size so
1258	// DDx-authored beads always round-trip through `bd import`. Empirically
1259	// validated against bd 1.0.2 on 2026-04-20: 65,535 accepts, 65,536 rejects.
1260	const MaxFieldBytes = 65535
1261	
1262	// capFieldBytes enforces MaxFieldBytes on a single field value. Callers that
1263	// need to preserve the full payload should persist it to a sidecar artifact
1264	// and store a path reference in the field; this function is the defense-in-
1265	// depth cap for code paths that don't. Returns the input unchanged when it
1266	// fits; otherwise returns head+tail truncation with a byte-count marker.
1267	func capFieldBytes(s string) string {
1268		if len(s) <= MaxFieldBytes {
1269			return s
1270		}
1271		// Reserve a marker that always fits; keep head heavy (2/3) so
1272		// human-readable context is preserved for the common short-rationale case.
1273		marker := fmt.Sprintf("\n…[truncated, %d bytes]\n", len(s))
1274		budget := MaxFieldBytes - len(marker)
1275		if budget <= 0 {
1276			return s[:MaxFieldBytes]
1277		}
1278		head := (budget * 2) / 3
1279		tail := budget - head
1280		return s[:head] + marker + s[len(s)-tail:]
1281	}
1282	
1283	// Cancel-marker keys on Bead.Extra. ADR-022 §Cancel SLA: an operator-initiated
1284	// cancel writes cancel-requested:true; the worker that honors the cancel writes
1285	// cancel-honored:true alongside it.
1286	const (
1287		ExtraCancelRequested = "cancel-requested"
1288		ExtraCancelHonored   = "cancel-honored"
1289	)
1290	
1291	// RequestCancel writes Extra[cancel-requested]=true on the bead. Idempotent:
1292	// if cancel-honored is already set the call is a silent no-op (the prior cancel
1293	// was already consumed). Returns true when the marker is now set on the bead
1294	// (either by this call or a prior one).
1295	func (s *Store) RequestCancel(id string) (bool, error) {
1296		var set bool
1297		err := s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1298			if b.Extra == nil {
1299				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1300			}
1301			if isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored]) {
1302				set = isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested])
1303				return
1304			}
1305			b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested] = true
1306			set = true
1307		})
1308		return set, err
1309	}
1310	
1311	// IsCancelRequested reports whether the bead carries an unconsumed cancel
1312	// marker (cancel-requested:true and cancel-honored not yet set).
1313	func (s *Store) IsCancelRequested(id string) (bool, error) {
1314		b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id)
1315		if err != nil {
1316			return false, err
1317		}
1318		if b == nil || b.Extra == nil {
1319			return false, nil
1320		}
1321		if isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored]) {
1322			return false, nil
1323		}
1324		return isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested]), nil
1325	}
1326	
1327	// MarkCancelHonored sets Extra[cancel-honored]=true. Called by the worker once
1328	// it has aborted at the next safe point in response to a cancel request.
1329	func (s *Store) MarkCancelHonored(id string) error {
1330		return s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1331			if b.Extra == nil {
1332				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1333			}
1334			b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored] = true
1335		})
1336	}
1337	
1338	func isExtraTrue(v any) bool {
1339		switch x := v.(type) {
1340		case bool:
1341			return x
1342		case string:
1343			return x == "true"
1344		}
1345		return false
1346	}
1347	
1348	func (s *Store) AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error {
1349		if event.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
1350			event.CreatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
1351		}
1352		// Defense in depth: cap every event body regardless of caller. The
1353		// reviewer write path separately persists the full stream to an artifact
1354		// and emits a <=512-byte body; this cap catches anything else.
1355		event.Body = capFieldBytes(event.Body)
1356		event.Summary = capFieldBytes(event.Summary)
1357		// Two storage shapes are possible here: inline Extra["events"] for active
1358		// beads, and a sidecar attachment for closed/archived beads (ADR-004
1359		// attachment model). When the bead has been externalized we append to the
1360		// sidecar and leave the row untouched aside from updated_at.
1361		var appendedToAttachment bool
1362		if err := s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1363			if b.Extra == nil {
1364				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1365			}
1366			if hasEventsAttachment(b) {
1367				appendedToAttachment = true
1368				return
1369			}
1370			var events []BeadEvent
1371			if raw, ok := b.Extra["events"]; ok {
1372				events = decodeBeadEvents(raw)
1373			}
1374			events = append(events, event)
1375			b.Extra["events"] = encodeEventsForExtra(events)
1376		}); err != nil {
1377			return err
1378		}
1379		if !appendedToAttachment {
1380			return nil
1381		}
1382		// Append to the sidecar under the store lock so concurrent readers and
1383		// writers see a consistent events log.
1384		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1385			existing, err := s.readEventsAttachment(id)
1386			if err != nil {
1387				return err
1388			}
1389			existing = append(existing, event)
1390			return s.writeEventsAttachment(id, existing)
1391		})
1392	}
1393	
1394	// Events returns the bead's evidence history in insertion order. Reads are
1395	// transparent across storage shapes: inline Extra["events"] for active beads,
1396	// or the externalized sidecar referenced by Extra[events_attachment] once the
1397	// bead has been closed.
1398	func (s *Store) Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error) {
1399		b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id)
1400		if err != nil {
1401			return nil, err
1402		}
1403		events, err := s.eventsForBead(b)
1404		if err != nil {
1405			return nil, err
1406		}
1407		if len(events) == 0 {
1408			return []BeadEvent{}, nil
1409		}
1410		out := make([]BeadEvent, len(events))
1411		copy(out, events)
1412		return out, nil
1413	}
1414	
1415	// EventsByKind returns all events for a bead filtered by kind, in insertion order.
1416	func (s *Store) EventsByKind(id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error) {
1417		all, err := s.Events(id)
1418		if err != nil {
1419			return nil, err
1420		}
1421		out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(all))
1422		for _, e := range all {
1423			if e.Kind == kind {
1424				out = append(out, e)
1425			}
1426		}
1427		return out, nil
1428	}
1429	
1430	func decodeBeadEvents(raw any) []BeadEvent {
1431		switch v := raw.(type) {
1432		case []BeadEvent:
1433			out := make([]BeadEvent, len(v))
1434			copy(out, v)
1435			return out
1436		case []any:
1437			out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(v))
1438			for _, item := range v {
1439				m, ok := item.(map[string]any)
1440				if !ok {
1441					continue
1442				}
1443				out = append(out, beadEventFromMap(m))
1444			}
1445			return out
1446		case []map[string]any:
1447			out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(v))
1448			for _, item := range v {
1449				out = append(out, beadEventFromMap(item))
1450			}
1451			return out
1452		default:
1453			return nil
1454		}
1455	}
1456	
1457	func beadEventFromMap(m map[string]any) BeadEvent {
1458		e := BeadEvent{}
1459		if v, ok := m["kind"].(string); ok {
1460			e.Kind = v
1461		}
1462		if v, ok := m["summary"].(string); ok {
1463			e.Summary = v
1464		}
1465		if v, ok := m["body"].(string); ok {
1466			e.Body = v
1467		}
1468		if v, ok := m["actor"].(string); ok {
1469			e.Actor = v
1470		}
1471		if v, ok := m["source"].(string); ok {
1472			e.Source = v
1473		}
1474		if v, ok := m["created_at"].(string); ok {
1475			if parsed, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v); err == nil {
1476				e.CreatedAt = parsed
1477			}
1478		}
1479		return e
1480	}
1481	
1482	// lastReviewVerdictFromEvents returns the verdict string from the last
1483	// kind:review event in the list. Returns "" when no review event exists.
1484	func lastReviewVerdictFromEvents(events []BeadEvent) string {
1485		verdict := ""
1486		for _, e := range events {
1487			if e.Kind != "review" {
1488				continue
1489			}
1490			v := strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(e.Summary))
1491			switch v {
1492			case "APPROVE", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION":
1493				verdict = v
1494			}
1495		}
1496		return verdict
1497	}
1498	
1499	// Close sets a bead's status to closed. When the close succeeds and the bead
1500	// carries an inline event history, those events are moved to a sidecar
1501	// attachment under .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl so the active row stays
1502	// small (per ADR-004's attachment model and TD-027 §c).
1503	func (s *Store) Close(args ...any) error {
1504		ctx := storeCallContext(args)
1505		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
1506			return err
1507		}
1508		var id string
1509		for _, arg := range args {
1510			if s, ok := arg.(string); ok {
1511				id = s
1512				break
1513			}
1514		}
1515		if id == "" {
1516			return fmt.Errorf("bead: close requires bead id")
1517		}
1518		// Capture last review verdict before closure for reviewer-accuracy tracking.
1519		// If the operator manually closes a BLOCK-reviewed bead, that contradicts
1520		// the reviewer's verdict (potential false-positive).
1521		verdictWas := ""
1522		if events, err := s.Events(id); err == nil {
1523			verdictWas = lastReviewVerdictFromEvents(events)
1524		}
1525	
1526		if err := s.SetLifecycleStatus(id, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1527			ManualClose: true,
1528			Reason:      "manual close",
1529			Source:      "Store.Close",
1530		}); err != nil {
1531			return err
1532		}
1533		if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
1534			return err
1535		}
1536		// Emit accuracy override when operator closes a BLOCK-reviewed bead.
1537		// AppendEvent handles sidecar append for already-externalized events.
1538		if verdictWas == "BLOCK" {
1539			_ = s.AppendEvent(id, BeadEvent{
1540				Kind:      "review-accuracy-override",
1541				Summary:   "BLOCK verdict contradicted by operator close",
1542				Body:      "verdict_was=BLOCK\noperator_action=close\nreason=manual close",
1543				Source:    "Store.Close",
1544				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1545			})
1546		}
1547		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1548		s.maybeOpportunisticMaintenance()
1549	
1550		// Cascade-close eligible beads superseded by X, then walk up one hop
1551		// to close dead-intermediate (execution-eligible==false) beads.
1552		// Both operations share a visited set to prevent cycles within Close().
1553		visited := map[string]bool{id: true}
1554		_ = s.cascadeAndWalkUp(id, visited)
1555	
1556		return nil
1557	}
1558	
1559	// externalizeEvents moves a bead's inline events into its sidecar attachment
1560	// under a fresh lock. Safe to call repeatedly — no-op when there are no
1561	// inline events (already externalized, or none recorded).
1562	func (s *Store) externalizeEvents(id string) error {
1563		if s.axonGraphQLActive() {
1564			return nil
1565		}
1566		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1567			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1568			if err != nil {
1569				return err
1570			}
1571			for i := range beads {
1572				if beads[i].ID != id {
1573					continue
1574				}
1575				if err := s.externalizeEventsInPlace(&beads[i]); err != nil {
1576					return err
1577				}
1578				return s.WriteAll(beads)
1579			}
1580			return nil
1581		})
1582	}
1583	
1584	// cascadeAndWalkUp performs both cascade-close (RC-2) and walk-up (RC-3) cleanup
1585	// within a single Close() invocation, sharing the visited set to prevent cycles.
1586	// First cascades close beads superseded by closedID, then walks up one hop to
1587	// check if the closed bead's parent should be auto-closed as a dead-intermediate.
1588	func (s *Store) cascadeAndWalkUp(closedID string, visited map[string]bool) error {
1589		all, err := s.ReadAll(context.Background())
1590		if err != nil {
1591			return err
1592		}
1593	
1594		// RC-2: Cascade-close superseded beads
1595		for _, x := range all {
1596			if visited[x.ID] {
1597				continue
1598			}
1599			if !s.canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(&x, closedID, all) {
1600				continue
1601			}
1602			visited[x.ID] = true
1603			_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(x.ID, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1604				ManualClose: true,
1605				Reason:      fmt.Sprintf("cascade-close superseded by %s", closedID),
1606				Source:      "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1607			})
1608			_ = s.externalizeEvents(x.ID)
1609			_ = s.AppendEvent(x.ID, BeadEvent{
1610				Kind:      "superseded_close",
1611				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("closed as superseded via %s", closedID),
1612				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("closed_by_cascade_of: %s\nreason: closed_as_superseded_via:%s", closedID, closedID),
1613				Source:    "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1614				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1615			})
1616			_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(x.ID)
1617		}
1618	
1619		// RC-3: Walk up one hop to close dead-intermediate (execution-eligible==false) beads
1620		closedBead := findBeadByID(all, closedID)
1621		if closedBead != nil && closedBead.Parent != "" {
1622			if !visited[closedBead.Parent] {
1623				s.walkUpClosureCandidate(closedBead.Parent, all, visited)
1624			}
1625		}
1626	
1627		return nil
1628	}
1629	
1630	// walkUpClosureCandidate checks if parent should be auto-closed when all its
1631	// children reach terminal state. Two closure paths:
1632	//   - Dead-intermediate: parent has execution-eligible=false; auto-closes once
1633	//     all children are terminal (RC-3 walk-up closure).
1634	//   - Epic auto-close: parent is an epic bead; auto-closes when every child is
1635	//     closed or cancelled (both count as terminal).
1636	//
1637	// Cancelled children are treated as terminal for both paths.
1638	// Single-hop only; does not chase the grandparent.
1639	func (s *Store) walkUpClosureCandidate(parentID string, allBeads []Bead, visited map[string]bool) {
1640		parent := findBeadByID(allBeads, parentID)
1641		if parent == nil {
1642			return
1643		}
1644	
1645		// Count children; closed AND cancelled both count as terminal.
1646		nonTerminalChildCount := 0
1647		totalChildCount := 0
1648		for _, b := range allBeads {
1649			if b.Parent == parentID {
1650				totalChildCount++
1651				if b.Status != StatusClosed && b.Status != StatusCancelled {
1652					nonTerminalChildCount++
1653				}
1654			}
1655		}
1656	
1657		if nonTerminalChildCount > 0 || totalChildCount == 0 {
1658			return
1659		}
1660	
1661		executionEligible, executionEligibleKnown := lifecycleExecutionEligible(*parent)
1662		isDeadIntermediate := executionEligibleKnown && !executionEligible
1663		isEpic := isEpicBead(*parent)
1664	
1665		if !isDeadIntermediate && !isEpic {
1666			return
1667		}
1668	
1669		var eventKind, reason, summary, body string
1670		if isDeadIntermediate {
1671			reason = "auto-close dead-intermediate (all children closed, not execution-eligible)"
1672			eventKind = "dead_intermediate_close"
1673			summary = "closed as dead-intermediate bead (all children closed, execution-eligible=false)"
1674			body = fmt.Sprintf("closed_because: all_children_closed\nexecution_eligible: false\ntotal_children: %d", totalChildCount)
1675		} else {
1676			reason = "auto-close epic: all children reached terminal state"
1677			eventKind = "epic_auto_close"
1678			summary = "auto-closed: all children reached terminal state"
1679			body = fmt.Sprintf("closed_because: all_children_terminal\ntotal_children: %d", totalChildCount)
1680		}
1681	
1682		visited[parentID] = true
1683		_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(parentID, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1684			ManualClose: true,
1685			Reason:      reason,
1686			Source:      "Store.Close.walkUpClosureCandidate",
1687		})
1688		_ = s.externalizeEvents(parentID)
1689		_ = s.AppendEvent(parentID, BeadEvent{
1690			Kind:      eventKind,
1691			Summary:   summary,
1692			Body:      body,
1693			Source:    "Store.Close.walkUpClosureCandidate",
1694			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1695		})
1696		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(parentID)
1697	}
1698	
1699	// EpicClosureCandidates returns open epic beads whose children have all reached
1700	// a terminal state (closed or cancelled). Useful for backfill-closing epics that
1701	// predate auto-close, via ddx bead reap --apply.
1702	func (s *Store) EpicClosureCandidates(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error) {
1703		all, err := s.ReadAll(ctx)
1704		if err != nil {
1705			return nil, err
1706		}
1707		childCount := make(map[string]int)
1708		nonTerminalCount := make(map[string]int)
1709		for _, b := range all {
1710			if b.Parent == "" {
1711				continue
1712			}
1713			childCount[b.Parent]++
1714			if b.Status != StatusClosed && b.Status != StatusCancelled {
1715				nonTerminalCount[b.Parent]++
1716			}
1717		}
1718		var candidates []Bead
1719		for _, b := range all {
1720			if b.Status == StatusClosed || b.Status == StatusCancelled {
1721				continue
1722			}
1723			if childCount[b.ID] == 0 {
1724				continue
1725			}
1726			if nonTerminalCount[b.ID] > 0 {
1727				continue
1728			}
1729			if !isEpicBead(b) {
1730				continue
1731			}
1732			candidates = append(candidates, b)
1733		}
1734		return candidates, nil
1735	}
1736	
1737	// cascadeCloseSuperseeded scans for open beads X where superseded-by==Y,
1738	// checks strict scope guards, and closes eligible ones. One-hop deep only;
1739	// if X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's closure is deferred.
1740	// Idempotent: re-running is a no-op for already-closed X.
1741	func (s *Store) cascadeCloseSuperseeded(closedID string) error {
1742		all, err := s.ReadAll(context.Background())
1743		if err != nil {
1744			return err
1745		}
1746		visited := map[string]bool{closedID: true}
1747		for _, x := range all {
1748			if visited[x.ID] {
1749				continue
1750			}
1751			if !s.canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(&x, closedID, all) {
1752				continue
1753			}
1754			visited[x.ID] = true
1755			_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(x.ID, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1756				ManualClose: true,
1757				Reason:      fmt.Sprintf("cascade-close superseded by %s", closedID),
1758				Source:      "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1759			})
1760			_ = s.externalizeEvents(x.ID)
1761			_ = s.AppendEvent(x.ID, BeadEvent{
1762				Kind:      "superseded_close",
1763				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("closed as superseded via %s", closedID),
1764				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("closed_by_cascade_of: %s\nreason: closed_as_superseded_via:%s", closedID, closedID),
1765				Source:    "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1766				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1767			})
1768			_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(x.ID)
1769		}
1770		return nil
1771	}
1772	
1773	// canCascadeCloseSuperseeded checks all scope guards before cascade-closing X.
1774	// All guards must pass for a bead to be eligible. Reads current state fresh.
1775	func (s *Store) canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(x *Bead, closedID string, allBeads []Bead) bool {
1776		// Re-read X's current state to ensure fresh snapshot
1777		fresh, err := s.Get(context.Background(), x.ID)
1778		if err != nil {
1779			return false
1780		}
1781	
1782		// Guard 1: X is open AND (X.superseded-by == closedID OR X carries label superseded-by:closedID) AND closedID is closed
1783		if fresh.Status != StatusOpen {
1784			return false
1785		}
1786		superseededBy := lifecycleSupersededBy(*fresh)
1787		if superseededBy != closedID {
1788			found := false
1789			for _, l := range fresh.Labels {
1790				if l == fmt.Sprintf("superseded-by:%s", closedID) {
1791					found = true
1792					break
1793				}
1794			}
1795			if !found {
1796				return false
1797			}
1798		}
1799		// Verify closedID is actually closed
1800		var closedBead *Bead
1801		for i, b := range allBeads {
1802			if b.ID == closedID {
1803				closedBead = &allBeads[i]
1804				break
1805			}
1806		}
1807		if closedBead == nil || closedBead.Status != StatusClosed {
1808			return false
1809		}
1810	
1811		// Guard 2: X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X)
1812		for _, z := range allBeads {
1813			if z.Status == StatusOpen && z.ID != fresh.ID {
1814				for _, d := range z.Dependencies {
1815					if d.DependsOnID == fresh.ID {
1816						return false
1817					}
1818				}
1819			}
1820		}
1821	
1822		// Guard 3: X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker
1823		for _, d := range fresh.Dependencies {
1824			target := findBeadByID(allBeads, d.DependsOnID)
1825			if target != nil && target.Status != StatusClosed && target.Status != StatusCancelled {
1826				return false
1827			}
1828		}
1829	
1830		// Guard 4: X has attempt_count == 0 (no execution-related events)
1831		events, _ := s.Events(fresh.ID)
1832		for _, e := range events {
1833			if strings.Contains(e.Kind, "execution") || strings.Contains(e.Kind, "dispatch") ||
1834				strings.Contains(e.Kind, "work") || e.Kind == "execute-bead" {
1835				return false
1836			}
1837		}
1838	
1839		// Guard 5: X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp
1840		// For simplicity, check if notes are present (indicates operator activity)
1841		if strings.TrimSpace(fresh.Notes) != "" {
1842			return false
1843		}
1844	
1845		// Guard 6: X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id)
1846		if fresh.Status == StatusInProgress {
1847			return false
1848		}
1849		if claimFresh, _, err := s.ClaimHeartbeatFresh(fresh.ID); err == nil && claimFresh {
1850			return false
1851		}
1852	
1853		// Guard 7: X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label
1854		for _, l := range fresh.Labels {
1855			switch l {
1856			case "manual-hold", "no-auto-close", "container":
1857				return false
1858			}
1859		}
1860	
1861		return true
1862	}
1863	
1864	func findBeadByID(beads []Bead, id string) *Bead {
1865		for i, b := range beads {
1866			if b.ID == id {
1867				return &beads[i]
1868			}
1869		}
1870		return nil
1871	}
1872	
1873	// ErrClosureGateRejected indicates a close was refused because the bead does
1874	// not carry the evidence required for an automated closure: a terminal
1875	// verdict event (review APPROVE with non-empty rationale, or an explicit
1876	// review-skipped / manual-close marker) AND an execution-evidence marker
1877	// (closing_commit_sha, session_id, or an execute-bead success event in the
1878	// events history).
1879	//
1880	// Automated work paths (execute-bead + reviewer) always go through
1881	// CloseWithEvidence. The plain Store.Close remains as the manual
1882	// administration escape hatch — it bypasses the gate by design (ddx-e30e60a9
1883	// acceptance §1).
1884	var ErrClosureGateRejected = fmt.Errorf("closure gate: insufficient evidence")
1885	
1886	// ClosureGate inspects a bead and returns nil iff the close is safe. It
1887	// rejects only the specific shapes that caused the 2026-04-18/20
1888	// review-malfunction incidents:
1889	//
1890	//  1. axon-c5cc071a (silent false-closure): closed with no events AND no
1891	//     closing_commit_sha. Rejected by the execution-evidence check —
1892	//     closing_commit_sha must be non-empty OR at least one event must exist.
1893	//  2. f7ae036f (broken APPROVE): a review event with summary=APPROVE whose
1894	//     body is empty. Rejected — APPROVE with no rationale is exactly the
1895	//     parse-mis-extract shape the reviewer bug produced.
1896	//
1897	// Beads without a review step (--no-review, no Reviewer configured, already-
1898	// satisfied paths) pass the gate as long as they carry execution evidence.
1899	// This keeps the surface small: the gate catches known-bad shapes, not every
1900	// conceivable edge case. Additional invariants belong in future dedicated
1901	// beads so the rejection reason stays auditable.
1902	func ClosureGate(b *Bead) error {
1903		if b == nil {
1904			return fmt.Errorf("%w: nil bead", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1905		}
1906		hasClosingCommit := false
1907		if sha, ok := b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"].(string); ok && strings.TrimSpace(sha) != "" {
1908			hasClosingCommit = true
1909		}
1910		events := decodeBeadEvents(b.Extra["events"])
1911		// Externalized events still count as evidence for the gate. We only need
1912		// to know whether *some* event history exists, not iterate it; the
1913		// gate's APPROVE-with-empty-rationale check below relies on inline events
1914		// (the verdict is recorded before externalization).
1915		hasAttachedEvents := hasEventsAttachment(b)
1916		// Reject the axon-c5cc071a shape: no events AND no closing commit.
1917		if len(events) == 0 && !hasAttachedEvents && !hasClosingCommit {
1918			return fmt.Errorf("%w: no execution evidence (empty events and no closing_commit_sha)", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1919		}
1920		// Reject the f7ae036f shape: an APPROVE verdict with empty rationale.
1921		for _, e := range events {
1922			if e.Kind == "review" && strings.EqualFold(strings.TrimSpace(e.Summary), "APPROVE") {
1923				if strings.TrimSpace(e.Body) == "" {
1924					return fmt.Errorf("%w: review APPROVE with empty rationale (malformed reviewer verdict)", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1925				}
1926			}
1927		}
1928		return nil
1929	}
1930	
1931	// CloseWithEvidence sets a bead's status to closed and records agent session evidence.
1932	// sessionID is the agent session that completed the work.
1933	// commitSHA is the exact closing commit SHA when it is known.
1934	//
1935	// Enforces ClosureGate (ddx-e30e60a9): a bead cannot transition to closed
1936	// via this path without a terminal verdict event plus execution evidence.
1937	// Store.Close is the manual-administration escape hatch when the gate is
1938	// inappropriate.
1939	func (s *Store) CloseWithEvidence(id string, sessionID string, commitSHA string) error {
1940		if err := s.closeWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA); err != nil {
1941			return err
1942		}
1943		// Externalize events only when the gate actually transitioned the bead
1944		// to closed; rejected closes leave the bead open and inline.
1945		if b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id); err == nil && b != nil && b.Status == StatusClosed {
1946			if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
1947				return err
1948			}
1949		}
1950		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1951		s.maybeOpportunisticMaintenance()
1952		return nil
1953	}
1954	
1955	func (s *Store) closeWithEvidence(id string, sessionID string, commitSHA string) error {
1956		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
1957			if b.Extra == nil {
1958				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1959			}
1960			if sessionID != "" {
1961				b.Extra["session_id"] = sessionID
1962			}
1963			if commitSHA != "" {
1964				b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"] = commitSHA
1965			}
1966			// ClosureGate guards the evidence-close path only; reconcile-close skips
1967			// it by design (TD-031 §3.1).
1968			if err := ClosureGate(b); err != nil {
1969				// Surface via bead notes so a later operator audit can see why the
1970				// close was refused; a single error path would be dropped by the
1971				// Update callback signature (no error return).
1972				appendClosureRejectNote(b, err)
1973				return nil
1974			}
1975			return transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1976				ManualClose: true,
1977				Reason:      "close with evidence",
1978				Source:      "Store.CloseWithEvidence",
1979			})
1980		})
1981	}
1982	
1983	func appendClosureRejectNote(b *Bead, err error) {
1984		stamp := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
1985		note := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] closure rejected: %v", stamp, err)
1986		if b.Notes == "" {
1987			b.Notes = note
1988			return
1989		}
1990		b.Notes = b.Notes + "\n" + note
1991	}
1992	
1993	// AppendNotes appends operator-facing notes to an existing bead.
1994	func (s *Store) AppendNotes(id string, appendNotes string) error {
1995		appendNotes = strings.TrimSpace(appendNotes)
1996		if appendNotes == "" {
1997			return nil
1998		}
1999		return s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
2000			if b.Notes != "" {
2001				b.Notes = b.Notes + "\n\n" + appendNotes
2002				return
2003			}
2004			b.Notes = appendNotes
2005		})
2006	}
2007	
2008	// Reopen sets a bead's status back to open, clears claim fields, optionally
2009	// appends notes, and records an immutable reopen event — all in one atomic
2010	// lock acquisition.
2011	func (s *Store) Reopen(id string, reason string, appendNotes string) error {
2012		now := time.Now().UTC()
2013		return s.WithLock(func() error {
2014			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
2015			if err != nil {
2016				return err
2017			}
2018			found := false
2019			for i := range beads {
2020				if beads[i].ID != id {
2021					continue
2022				}
2023				b := &beads[i]
2024				if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, StatusOpen, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
2025					ManualReopen: true,
2026				}); err != nil {
2027					return err
2028				}
2029				b.Owner = ""
2030				b.UpdatedAt = now
2031				if b.Extra == nil {
2032					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
2033				}
2034				// Clear claim fields
2035				clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra)
2036				// Append notes
